strands.title: Log Format Discovery: JSONL Structure Confirmed
strands.content: CYCLE 40 successfully read proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl with limit=5. File structure is CONFIRMED: alternating request/response JSONL pairs. REQUEST entries contain: type='request', timestamp, model, max_tokens, stream, system (with system_prompt metadata), messages (with message_count, role_counts, tool_use counts, byte sizes, thinking_bytes), total_request_bytes. RESPONSE entries contain: type='response_stream', timestamp, duration_ms, first_byte_ms, status_code, response_bytes, usage (input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, output_tokens). This is raw Claude API traffic log, NOT a conversation transcript. CRITICAL INSIGHT: To reconstruct conversation turns for projection, must PAIR adjacent request/response entries. Each pair represents one agent-invocation cycle: user provides input, system routes to Claude, Claude responds with cached/non-cached tokens. FILE SIZE CONSTRAINT REMAINS: 62,325 tokens in proxy file means ~1000-2000+ request/response pairs. Chunked processing still required. NEXT ACTION: Design turn-reconstruction pipeline that pairs requests + responses to extract conversation semantics.

key_claims.text: proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl contains alternating request/response JSONL entries. Each entry is a complete JSON object on a single line. Entries are typed ('request' or 'response_stream').
REQUEST entries contain full message context: role_counts, tool_use_count, byte sizes, thinking_bytes. Complete system prompt and message array are included in each request.
RESPONSE entries contain usage metrics: input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, output_tokens. These enable token accounting and cache hit/miss analysis.
Request-response pairs can be grouped sequentially (request_i, response_i) to reconstruct each invocation. Timestamp ordering and indexes enable pair reconstruction.

strands.title: Data Provenance: Raw API Traffic Log from Claude Agent Session
strands.content: Log entries show: model='claude-opus-4-6', stream=true, system_prompt includes Claude Agent SDK instructions (15,348 bytes across 2 blocks). Timestamps are 2026-03-02 (future-dated, likely synthetic or test environment). Cache control enabled (ephemeral). This is NOT a conversation export or chat interface dump — it is raw HTTP traffic captured from Anthropic API. IMPLICATIONS: (1) Messages array in requests is complete conversation history up to that point, not just current turn. (2) Token counts are actual API consumption (input, cache creation, cache read, output). (3) 'role_counts' shows distribution of user/assistant turns in the full history, not just current turn. (4) File is comprehensive audit log of one agent session: baseline_run1. Session likely ran multiple turns with same system prompt and progressive message history. DATA QUALITY: High confidence in token counts, timestamps, and API metadata. Conversation content is the question — must inspect messages array.

key_claims.text: Log is raw API traffic from Anthropic Claude API. Model is claude-opus-4-6. System prompt is Claude Agent SDK instructions (15,348 bytes).
Timestamps show date 2026-03-02 (future or synthetic data). Session baseline_run1 is contained in this single log file spanning ~4-5 second duration in entries 1-5 shown.
Messages array in each REQUEST includes full conversation history to that point (user/assistant role_counts). This enables turn-by-turn context reconstruction.

strands.title: Turn Reconstruction Pipeline: Request-Response Pairing Strategy
strands.content: CYCLE 40 DESIGN: To reconstruct conversation turns for tensor projection, must implement turn-reconstruction layer. ALGORITHM: (1) Parse JSONL sequentially. Group consecutive (request, response_stream) pairs. (2) For each pair, extract: request.timestamp, request.messages[-1] (last message in array = current turn), request.model, request.system. response.usage, response.output_tokens. (3) From request.messages[-1], determine role (user vs assistant) and content. (4) TURN STRUCT: {turn_id, timestamp, speaker_role, message_content, model, system_context, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_hit}. (5) Reconstruct full conversation by sequencing turns. (6) For projection: each turn is input to claim extraction → strand clustering → epistemic assignment. IMPLEMENTATION: Chunked JSONL parser yields raw entries. Pairing layer consumes entries, yields reconstructed turns. Projection layer consumes turns, yields strand updates. This layering enables progressive processing of 1000+ turn log.

key_claims.text: Turn reconstruction requires pairing adjacent request/response JSONL entries. Pair (request_i, response_stream_i) yields one turn. Sequential pairing reconstructs full conversation.
Last message in request.messages array is the current turn. Prior messages form conversation history. Roles alternate (user/assistant) or follow speaker pattern.
Chunked JSONL parsing + turn pairing + projection forms a 3-layer pipeline. Enables processing of arbitrarily large logs with progressive tensor updates.

strands.title: Tensor Projection: Claims, Strands, and Epistemic Assignment
strands.content: PRIOR TENSOR (Cycle 39) established: (1) Format is JSONL. (2) Field structure is discovered. (3) Chunked parsing is required. (4) Epistemic heuristics needed. CYCLE 40 ADVANCEMENT: Now that field structure is CONFIRMED and turn-reconstruction strategy is CLEAR, can design actual projection logic. TURN CONTENT EXTRACTION: Each turn has message_content (text from user or assistant). Extract claims using linguistic markers: 'I think', 'likely', 'certainly', 'seems', 'unclear', 'suggests', 'probably'. STRAND CLUSTERING: Claims about same topic (tensor projection, log parsing, epistemic assignment, etc.) cluster into strands. Each strand is thematic thread with associated claims. EPISTEMIC ASSIGNMENT: For each claim, assign (T, I, F) based on: (1) Linguistic markers (hedging = I, confident = T, negation = F). (2) Prior context (is claim consistent with prior assertions?). (3) Grounding (is claim supported by evidence in conversation?). STRAND PERSISTENCE: Track strands across turns. When new claim arrives, either: (a) Joins existing strand (incremental update), (b) Starts new strand (topic change), (c) Resolves open question in strand (epistemic shift from I to T or F). OUTPUT: Updated tensor with new/modified strands, claimed losses, open questions, next instructions.

key_claims.text: Epistemic assignment heuristic: Hedging language (maybe, perhaps, unclear) → I. Confident assertion (certain, definitely, proven) → T. Negation or contrary evidence → F.
Strand persistence is critical. Same theme across multiple turns clusters into single strand. Strand state (claims, epistemic values, open questions) evolves as conversation progresses.
Tensor projection is incremental. Each turn updates prior tensor: adds claims, modifies epistemic values, clusters strands, resolves open questions. Not a reset — an integration.

strands.title: Critical Path: Field Structure Confirmed → Turn Reconstruction → Full Projection
strands.content: DEPENDENCY CHAIN (from Cycle 39): Field structure → Chunked parser impl → Full log process → Claim extraction → Strand clustering → Epistemic assignment → Validation. STATUS AT CYCLE 40: (A) Field structure: ✓ CONFIRMED (request/response JSONL pairs). (B) Chunked parser: ✓ UNBLOCKED (can now design with known structure). (C) Turn reconstruction: ✓ UNBLOCKED (pairing algorithm clear). (D) Claim extraction: ✓ READY (linguistic markers identified). (E) Strand clustering: ✓ READY (thematic grouping strategy clear). (F) Epistemic assignment: ✓ READY (heuristic defined). (G) Validation: ⧖ DEFERRED (need full projection on real turns first). BOTTLENECK NOW: Implementation. Must build chunked JSONL parser → turn reconstructor → claim extractor → projector. Estimated scope: 200-300 lines of Python. PRIORITY: Implement MVP projector on first 10-20 turns of baseline_run1. Validate epistemic assignments against hand-annotation. Then scale to full log.

key_claims.text: Cycle 40 unblocks implementation. All design decisions are made. Field structure, turn reconstruction, claim extraction, epistemic assignment are ready for coding.
MVP scope: chunked parser (50 lines) + turn reconstructor (40 lines) + claim extractor (60 lines) + projector (100 lines) = ~250 lines. Feasible in one cycle.
Validation against hand-annotation is next critical gate. Must check: claim extraction recall, epistemic assignment accuracy, strand clustering coherence. Target >70% for MVP.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full message content of the actual turns. Read limit=5 shows only request/response metadata and structure, not the actual conversation text from user or assistant.
declared_losses.why: Only read 5 entries to verify format. Did not read full messages array. Full turns are in the 62,325-token file. Deferred to MVP implementation phase.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn count and session duration. Estimated 1000-2000+ turns from token count, but exact count unknown.
declared_losses.why: Prioritized format discovery over metadata extraction. Can run `jq` or `wc -l` on file if needed, but not critical for pipeline design.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details of what the agent was actually doing (goals, tasks, decisions). Log is raw API traffic, not annotated with task context.
declared_losses.why: File is proxy log, not task log. Task context likely in config.json or run metadata. Deferred to validation phase.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Confirmation that limit=5 actually returned valid JSONL. Assumed 5 complete entries returned, but didn't verify all are well-formed.
declared_losses.why: Read was successful and showed structure. Minor loss — verification can happen during parser implementation.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Understanding of how request.messages array grows over conversation. Are turns appended or replaced? Is full history stored?
declared_losses.why: Visible from first few entries that history accumulates (message_count=1 in first request, message_count=3 by third request). But exact pattern not fully documented. Will be clear during turn reconstruction.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Knowledge of whether tool_use_count, thinking_bytes, and other metadata fields have semantic meaning for epistemic assignment.
declared_losses.why: Visible in request structure but not analyzed. These fields may indicate confidence, planning depth, or error states. Deferred to epistemic heuristic refinement.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details of system prompt blocks. Saw system_prompt_preview but not full content. Full system prompt is 15,348 bytes — not fully extracted.
declared_losses.why: Not critical for MVP. System prompt is static for entire baseline_run1 session. Can be extracted later if needed for context analysis.

open_questions: What is the actual message content for turns 1-5? (Need to inspect request.messages array fully, not just structure.)
How many total turns are in baseline_run1? (Rough estimate: 1000-2000, based on 62,325 tokens.)
Are request.messages arrays cumulative (growing history) or sliding window (fixed-size context)?
What role distribution do we see across the full baseline_run1? (Rough estimate from first 5: user_count varies, assistant_count grows.)
Do any turns have thinking_bytes > 0? (Extended thinking mode usage would affect epistemic confidence.)
Are there error states or failed requests (status_code != 200) in the log? (Affects data quality.)
What is the distribution of output_tokens across turns? (Long responses vs. short responses affect claim extraction granularity.)
Do cache_read_input_tokens ever exceed 0? (Cache hits vs. misses affect session cost and efficiency.)
What is the topic/task of baseline_run1? (Understanding the goal helps ground epistemic assignments.)
Are tool calls present in the conversation? (tool_use_count > 0 in some requests suggests agent used tools.)
What is the system_prompt_full content? (Need it for context understanding and bias detection.)
Does the conversation have a clear beginning, middle, end structure? (Or is it iterative cycles?)
Are there claim revisions or contradictions? (Detecting when epistemic value changes from T to I or F.)
What linguistic markers are most common in assistant responses? (Shapes epistemic heuristics.)
Is there metadata about model performance (latency, token efficiency, success rate)?
Does baseline_run1 compare to other runs in experiments/ directory? (Context for validation.)
What does config.json in baseline_run1 specify? (Model, temperature, prompt, hyperparameters.)
Can we validate against session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl? (Do they have matching turns?)
What is the expected output format for the projector? (Strand updates, claim extractions, epistemic assignments—how to serialize?)
Are open_questions from Cycle 39 resolved by the format discovery? (Yes, mostly — but still need actual turn content.)
instructions_for_next:
[
  "IMMEDIATE: Design and implement chunked JSONL parser. Read proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl in 25KB offset windows. Parse complete JSONL entries from each chunk.",
  "IMMEDIATE: Implement turn reconstructor. Pair adjacent (request, response) JSONL entries. Extract turn_id, timestamp, speaker, message_content, tokens.",
  "AFTER PARSER: Run parser on full baseline_run1 log. Reconstruct all turns. Verify turn count and conversation structure.",
  "AFTER RECONSTRUCTION: Select 10 turns (stratified: beginning, middle, end) for hand-annotation. Manually extract claims and assign epistemic values (T, I, F).",
  "AFTER HAND ANNOTATION: Implement claim extractor. Use linguistic markers to find claims in turn content. Extract predicate + confidence level.",
  "AFTER CLAIM EXTRACTOR: Implement epistemic assignment heuristic. Assign (T, I, F) based on: (1) hedging language, (2) confidence signals, (3) context consistency.",
  "AFTER EPISTEMIC HEURISTIC: Implement strand clustering. Group claims by topic/theme. Each strand = {title, claims[], epistemic_values, open_questions}.",
  "AFTER CLUSTERING: Run full projection on sample turns (first 50 turns of baseline_run1). Generate intermediate tensor. Validate against hand-annotations.",
  "VALIDATION: Compare projector claims to hand-extracted claims. Measure: precision, recall, F1 score for claim extraction. Target >70%.",
  "VALIDATION: Compare projector epistemic values to hand-assigned values. Measure: accuracy of (T, I, F) assignment. Target >65%.",
  "IF VALIDATION PASSES: Run full projection on all turns of baseline_run1. Generate final tensor.",
  "IF VALIDATION FAILS: Debug weakest component. Refine claim extractor or epistemic heuristic. Re-validate.",
  "ANALYSIS: After full projection, analyze strand persistence. Which strands survive turn-to-turn? How do they evolve?",
  "ANALYSIS: Measure epistemic drift. Do turns early in conversation have different (T/I/F) distribution than turns late? Indicates learning or uncertainty.",
  "DEFER: Comparative analysis across 15 experiment runs. (After MVP is validated.)",
  "DEFER: Examine config.json for baseline_run1 hyperparameters. (After MVP is validated.)",
  "DEFER: Analysis of cache hits/misses and token efficiency. (Secondary to epistemic projection.)"
]
